name: apep (serpent of chaos
shortDescription: Apep, also known as Apophis, was the ancient Egyptian serpent deity representing chaos, darkness, and destruction. He was the eternal enemy of the sun god Ra, constantly battling to plunge the world into darkness.
description: Apep, also known as Apophis, was a massive serpent and a powerful deity in ancient Egyptian mythology. He embodied chaos, darkness, and non-being, and was the antithesis of Ma'at (truth, order, and cosmic balance). Apep's origin is often linked to the primordial waters of Nun, representing the formless void that existed before creation. Every night, as Ra, the sun god, journeyed through the underworld (Duat) in his solar barque, Apep would lie in wait, attempting to swallow him or capsize his boat, plunging the world into eternal darkness. The Egyptians believed that the daily sunrise was a victory for Ra and a defeat for Apep, ensured through the prayers and rituals performed by priests and ordinary people. These rituals, often involving spells, incantations, and the destruction of images of Apep, were crucial in maintaining cosmic order. Apep was not worshipped in the traditional sense, as he was a force of evil to be repelled rather than venerated. His existence was a constant threat, emphasizing the fragility of creation and the ongoing struggle between order and chaos. Though no temples were dedicated to him, his significance lay in his role as the ultimate antagonist in the cosmic drama of Egyptian mythology. He is often depicted as a giant serpent, sometimes with other monstrous features, and represents the untamed, destructive forces of the universe.

sources.title: The Complete Gods and Goddesses of Ancient Egypt
sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
